description:
Transform this into a bold, modern Founder's Office portfolio for Keerthan Singhvi. Design direction: Editorial bold × Product hybrid - large expressive typography, strong layout composition, confident spacing, product-grade polish. Use a distinctive accent color (not default blue). Create balanced, centered layout (1100-1300px max-width) with multi-column sections where appropriate. Avoid over-left-alignment and dead space.

Hero Section:
- Large, bold headline: 'Founder's Office operator turning ambiguity into systems, momentum, and outcomes.'
- Below headline: rotating micro-quotes with smooth animation (Strategy brain. Operator hands. / Chaos → Clarity → Shipping. / I build leverage, not decks. / If it's messy, I'm interested. / Systems over slogans.)
- Primary CTA: LinkedIn (https://linkedin.com/in/keerthan-singhvi)
- Secondary CTA: Download Resume (link to resume PDF at /a3749d42-c3ec-4634-8e3d-3ab25723c3f8.pdf)
- Strong visual hierarchy, balanced composition, visually engaging

About Section:
- Title: 'The Operator'
- Write sharp, punchy copy with operator voice:
  * Founder's Office at Contineu (construction-tech AI startup)
  * Built zero-to-one systems across CS, procurement, GTM, product
  * High ownership, cross-functional, thrives in ambiguity
  * Finance background with execution mindset
  * Turns strategy into shipable outcomes
- Short blocks, light professional humor, no buzzwords
- Strong visual design with depth

Experience Section:
- Parse data from resume PDF (Contineu Founder's Office, Kala The Art, Puzzles Living internship, Christ University activities)
- Card-based layout with rich visuals
- Show role, duration, key achievements with metrics
- Scannable, strong hierarchy
- Interactive hover states
- Skill tags integrated visually

Case Studies Section (Critical):
- Create 3-4 expandable case study cards based on Contineu achievements:
  1. Customer Success & Implementation System (trained 15+ engineers, 100% standardization)
  2. Global Procurement Optimization (INR 7L savings, 3+ international imports)
  3. Go-to-Market & Expansion (U.S. compliance, 300+ qualified prospects CRM)
  4. Cross-functional Operations Leadership (finance, compliance, recruitment)
- Each card shows: Problem, Execution, Outcomes, Learnings
- Click-to-expand interaction with animated transitions
- Rich card visuals, clear affordances
- Visual depth and separation

Skills & Tooling Section:
- Visual badge/chip system
- Group by category (Operations, Analysis, GTM, Product, Soft Skills)
- Technical: SOPs/Playbooks, Customer Success, Procurement, Financial Analysis, Excel/Power BI, CRM, UI/UX Research
- Soft: Communication, Cross-functional Collaboration, Problem-Solving, Negotiation, Execution Ownership
- Easy to scan, avoid dense text

Resume Section:
- Clean framing
- Prominent download CTA for resume PDF

Contact Section:
- Strong CTA design
- LinkedIn primary (https://linkedin.com/in/keerthan-singhvi)
- Email: singhvikeerthan03@gmail.com
- Location: Bangalore, India
- Simple, bold design

Interactions:
- Smooth hover states on cards and CTAs
- Scroll-based section entrances (subtle)
- Refined quote rotation animation
- Micro-interactions for delight
- Light depth transitions (shadows, elevation)
- Premium, purposeful motion - no aggressive effects

Overall: Desktop-first, fully responsive, SEO optimized, fast, accessible. This should feel like a thoughtfully designed product - bold, confident, alive. Make recruiters immediately understand value and energy.
Fix the green dot indicator near 'Open to Opportunities' badge - ensure it's properly styled with bright green color (#00ff00 or similar) and is visible
Update Contineu dates: Change from 'June 2025 - Present' to 'June 2025 - December 2025' (role has ended)
Fix college education dates: Change Christ University dates from 'Aug 2022 - Apr 2025' to 'Aug 2022 - Mar 2025'
Fix the location card in the contact section - it has no UI/content visible. Add proper icon and text for 'Bangalore, India'

Add professional portrait image to the hero/landing section. Image path: /home/user/Images/keerthan-professional-portrait-8acTzhBl-spJBf4AA7uvc.png. Position it prominently in the hero section - use your design judgment for the best ratio and placement. Could be circular or with subtle border/frame. Should complement the bold headline and rotating quotes while adding personality and human connection to the page. Consider placing it on the right side of the hero in a balanced layout, or as a focal element integrated with the headline.

Remove the portrait image from the hero/landing section completely. Realign the hero section to center all content - headline, rotating quotes, and CTAs should be centered and balanced without the image. Adjust spacing and layout accordingly for a clean, impactful hero.

Rewrite the experience section to match resume exactly. Use these details from the resume:

Contineu - Founder's Office (Jun 2025 - Dec 2025, Bengaluru):
- Pioneered end-to-end Customer Success and Implementation function, establishing company's first SOPs and training 15+ engineers across 8 sites to achieve 100% process standardization
- Optimized global procurement and supply chain operations by negotiating vendor contracts, securing INR 7L in savings, and managing 3+ international imports with zero compliance or documentation errors
- Spearheaded go-to-market and expansion strategy by vetting U.S. compliance and banking partners and developing a CRM database of 300+ qualified prospects across domestic and international markets
- Directed cross-functional operations as Founder's primary execution lead, overseeing finance, compliance planning, and full-cycle recruitment to scale the core technical team
- Drove product roadmap enhancements by translating field research into UI/UX improvements, collaborating with engineering to implement multiple user workflow-driven feature upgrades

Kala The Art - Manager, Family Business (Apr 2018 - May 2025, Bengaluru):
- Managed end-to-end business operations, including financial oversight, inventory management, and customer relations, achieving consistent 6-figure revenue with a steady 15-20% increase in revenue YoY

Puzzles Living, Campus Binge - Intern (May 2024 - Jul 2024, Bengaluru):
- Led targeted campaigns that generated ₹20L in annual revenue and increased property occupancy by 20%
- Streamlined operations and inventory workflows across multiple properties using Excel, Airtable, and Power BI to boost efficiency and reporting accuracy

Double-check all dates match the resume.

In the contact section: Make email display in a single line (no wrapping). Remove timezone info (IST UTC+5:30) and the 📍 emoji icon from the location card - just show 'Bangalore, India' as plain text.

Redesign the 'Where I've Built' experience section with strict density control and visual balance:

LAYOUT & VISUAL REQUIREMENTS:
- All cards must have equal visual height and density - no card dominates
- Each card limited to: 1 context line, maximum 4 bullets, maximum 5 skill chips
- Increase internal padding and spacing within cards
- Improve typography hierarchy: strong title, clear role subtitle, subtle date treatment, high-contrast bullet text
- Use lighter card background (reduce visual heaviness)
- Maintain grid alignment and consistent rhythm
- Avoid dense paragraph blocks
- Keep it clean - depth belongs in case studies, not here

CONTENT UPDATES:

**Contineu** - Founder's Office | Jun 2025 – Dec 2025, Bengaluru
Context: Primary execution lead for construction-tech AI startup; owned Customer Success, procurement, GTM, and cross-functional operations.
Bullets (pick strongest 4):
- Pioneered Customer Success function; trained 15+ engineers across 8 sites, achieving 100% process standardization
- Optimized global procurement; negotiated vendor contracts, secured INR 7L savings, managed 3+ international imports with zero errors
- Spearheaded U.S. market expansion; vetted compliance partners, built CRM with 300+ qualified prospects
- Directed cross-functional operations; oversaw finance, compliance, recruitment to scale technical team
Skills: Customer Success • Procurement • GTM Strategy • Operations • Product

**Kala The Art** - Manager, Family Business | Apr 2018 – May 2025, Bengaluru
Context: End-to-end business operations for established art gallery and retail business.
Bullets:
- Managed financial oversight, inventory systems, and customer relations for 7+ years
- Achieved consistent 6-figure revenue with 15-20% YoY growth through operational excellence
Skills: Financial Management • Inventory Operations • Customer Relations • Business Strategy

**Puzzles Living** - Intern | May 2024 – Jul 2024, Bengaluru
Context: Operations and growth intern for campus housing startup.
Bullets:
- Led campaigns generating ₹20L annual revenue and 20% occupancy increase
- Streamlined multi-property workflows using Excel, Airtable, and Power BI
Skills: Growth Campaigns • Operations • Data Analysis • Workflow Optimization

**Christ University** - BBA Finance | Leadership & Sponsorship | Aug 2022 – Mar 2025
Context: Student leadership roles across sponsorship, training, and finance initiatives.
Bullets:
- Sponsorship Head for university management fest; owned revenue strategy and sponsor negotiations under increased pricing constraints
- Built and trained 25+ member sponsorship team with negotiation playbooks and outreach systems
- Secured marquee sponsors including The Hindu and Third Wave Coffee while meeting revenue targets
- Partner at A-Quant Finance Club; organized 5+ workshops, trained 80+ students in Excel and financial fundamentals
Skills: Sponsorship • Negotiation • Team Leadership • Training • Operations

DENSITY CONTROL (CRITICAL):
- If any card exceeds others in visual height, reduce bullets or shorten phrasing
- All cards must feel visually balanced
- Prioritize readability and scannability over completeness

OPTIONAL ENHANCEMENTS:
- Subtle hover elevation effect
- Micro-divider or accent line for separation
- Slight motion on hover for polish

Update the Kala The Art experience card with correct business description and new bullets:

**Kala The Art** - Manager, Family Business | Apr 2018 – May 2025, Bengaluru
Context: Family-run women's ethnic wear retail store.

Bullets (replace existing with these 4):
- Scaled a women's ethnic wear retail store to consistent 6-figure annual revenue with 15–20% YoY growth through better merchandising and vendor sourcing
- Owned end-to-end store operations including procurement, inventory planning, pricing, and daily execution
- Improved in-store conversion and repeat purchases by refining product mix and customer experience workflows
- Built basic operational discipline across billing, reporting, and stock visibility using lightweight tooling

Skills: Retail Operations • Inventory Management • Merchandising • Vendor Relations • Financial Management

Update the Puzzles Living experience card with 4 new bullets (replacing the current 2):

**Puzzles Living** - Intern | May 2024 – Jul 2024, Bengaluru
Context: Operations and growth intern for campus housing startup.

Bullets (replace existing with these 4):
- Generated ₹20L+ annualized revenue impact through targeted lead-generation experiments and on-ground demand validation
- Increased occupancy by ~20% by optimizing channel mix, follow-ups, and local partnerships
- Built lightweight reporting dashboards in Excel, Airtable, and Power BI for inventory and funnel visibility
- Supported pricing and competitive analysis to inform property-level growth decisions

Skills: Growth Campaigns • Operations • Data Analysis • Workflow Optimization • Pricing Strategy

Ensure all 4 experience cards now have exactly 4 bullets each and maintain equal visual height and density.

Update the About section ('The Operator') with new copy. Replace the entire About paragraph content with this exact text (use past tense for Contineu, no em dashes):

I've spent the last few years operating in environments where job descriptions are mostly fiction and real work lives somewhere between a half formed idea, a broken process, and a deadline that refuses to move. That's the zone I'm comfortable in.

Most recently, I worked in the Founder's Office at Contineu, a construction tech startup, building zero to one systems across customer success, procurement, GTM, and internal operations. If something needed structure, speed, or ownership, it usually landed on my plate.

My background is in finance, but my edge is execution. Strategy without shipping is just expensive thinking. I enjoy building leverage through systems that scale, processes that don't collapse under pressure, and documentation that people actually use.

High ownership, cross functional fluency, and a low tolerance for busywork. If there's a messy problem sitting between teams that no one wants to own, that's usually where you'll find me.

Preserve all typography, spacing, and layout styling - only change the text content.

Update the status line UI in the About section:

Replace the grey dot next to 'Bangalore, India' with a proper minimal location pin icon.

Icon requirements:
- Use a clean, minimal location pin/map marker icon (SVG)
- Match the site's icon style and stroke weight
- Use either black or the accent color (#e8793b) only - respect the 3-color system
- Align cleanly with text baseline
- Static, no animation
- Remove any decorative or meaningless dots entirely

Ensure the status line feels intentional and polished.

Replace the resume PDF file with the new version. Update the download link to point to the new PDF: /home/user/Attachments/cc52ff2a-3b25-4ef2-a601-2bf13ca9d68f.pdf. Do not change any website content, text, or layout - only swap the PDF file being linked/downloaded. The new PDF should be named 'Keerthan-Singhvi-Resume.pdf' or similar in the public folder.

Replace the entire About section ('The Operator') paragraph content with this exact new copy:

Most of the interesting things in my career started with low expectations and a lot of curiosity. Including my first real job.

I worked in the Founder's Office at a construction tech startup, where the role quickly expanded into building early systems across customer workflows, vendor coordination, GTM experiments, and internal operations. A lot of the work was zero to one. Creating structure where none existed, making decisions with imperfect information, and moving fast enough to learn.

My background is in finance, which shapes how I think about problems. I care about clarity, tradeoffs, and what actually moves the needle. In practice, my bias is toward execution. I enjoy building systems that scale cleanly, processes that hold up under pressure, and documentation that people genuinely use.

Somewhere along the way, getting comfortable with ambiguity became both a habit and a strength. If there's ambiguity, unclear ownership, or a problem sitting between teams, that's usually where I lean in.

Still a lot of figuring out left to do.

Preserve all typography, spacing, and layout styling - only change the text content.

Add a premium cursor-driven spotlight effect to the hero headline text ('Founder's Office operator turning ambiguity...').

Behavior:
- Soft circular light mask follows cursor when hovering near the headline text
- Spotlight radius: ~180-200px with feathered edges and smooth falloff
- Cursor lag: ~100ms easing for smooth trailing
- Subtly increases contrast/brightness of text under the spotlight
- Optional: very faint halo glow (low opacity, no heavy bloom)

Rules:
- Activates only when hovering the hero headline area
- No flashing, no aggressive glow, no color shifts
- Must feel premium and restrained, not game-like
- Smoothly fades out when cursor leaves the hero area
- Use CSS mask/clip-path or canvas/WebGL for performance

This should immediately communicate depth and interactivity.

Polish cursor and hover interactions across the entire UI:

Buttons:
- Subtle scale (2-4% max) or elevation on hover
- Soft easing (ease-out) in and out
- Clear hover affordance without heavy shadows
- Use transform: scale(1.02) or translateY(-2px)

Text links:
- Underline reveal or slight opacity shift on hover
- 150-250ms transitions
- Smooth easing curves

Cards (experience, case studies):
- Very light lift (2-3px translateY) or border emphasis on hover
- Avoid exaggerated motion
- Keep existing hover effects but refine timing and easing

Cursor:
- Consider custom cursor states for different interactive elements
- Ensure cursor changes appropriately (pointer on clickable elements)

All interactions should feel tactile, premium, and intentional.

Improve scroll experience without changing structure:

- Enable smooth scroll behavior globally (scroll-behavior: smooth or custom implementation)
- Add subtle fade + translate-in transitions when sections enter viewport
  - Use Intersection Observer API
  - Fade from opacity: 0 to 1
  - Translate from Y: 20-30px to 0
  - Stagger timing if multiple elements in a section
  - 400-600ms duration with ease-out

- Maintain spatial continuity between sections
- No snap scrolling or forced paging
- Scroll should feel guided and intentional, not mechanical
- Reduce any scroll jitter

Ensure all scroll animations are GPU-accelerated (transform and opacity only).

Micro-adjust spacing and layout for precision:

- Tighten excessive vertical gaps between hero elements (headline, rotating quotes, CTAs)
- Ensure visual balance horizontally (avoid dead zones on left/right)
- Improve alignment consistency between headline, buttons, and subtitle
- Normalize button padding so primary and secondary CTAs feel proportionate
- Ensure line lengths remain readable on wide screens (max-width checks)
- Review spacing between all major sections for consistency
- Check card padding consistency

No major layout restructuring - only micro-adjustments for polish and balance.

Visual cleanliness and performance optimization:

Visual:
- Reduce any overly strong background vignette or edge darkening if present
- Maintain high contrast but avoid crushed blacks
- Keep any grain/texture subtle
- Preserve strict color discipline (no color drift)
- Clean up any visual inconsistencies

Performance:
- Ensure all animations are GPU-accelerated (use transform and opacity)
- Avoid layout shifts during animation
- Keep transitions under 300ms unless intentionally slow
- Use consistent easing curves across all interactions (ease-out for exits, ease-in-out for toggles)
- Optimize any heavy elements
- Add will-change hints where appropriate for complex animations

The UI should feel 'finished' and production-ready.

Enhance hero typography and spotlight interaction:

1. Increase font sizes:
   - 'Keerthan Singhvi' name: increase font size moderately (from current size, add ~20-30% more)
   - 'Founder's Office operator...' headline: increase font size moderately (from current size, add ~15-20% more)
   - Don't go overboard - keep it balanced and readable

2. Extend spotlight effect to cover both the name AND the headline:
   - Spotlight should work when hovering over 'Keerthan Singhvi' OR the main headline
   - Increase spotlight brightness significantly (make it more visible and impactful)
   - Increase contrast boost under the spotlight

3. Add scroll-triggered zoom effect:
   - When user scrolls near the hero name area, add a subtle zoom/scale effect to 'Keerthan Singhvi'
   - Use Intersection Observer to detect when name is in/near viewport
   - Smooth scale animation (1.0 to 1.05 or similar)
   - Spotlight should remain visible during scroll interactions
   - Animation should feel smooth and premium

Add focus/zoom effect to experience cards in 'Where I've Built' section:

When user hovers over any experience card:
- The hovered card should scale up slightly (1.03-1.05) and get additional visual emphasis
- Other cards should scale down slightly (0.97-0.98) or reduce opacity slightly (0.5-0.6) to direct attention to the focused card
- Use smooth transitions (300-400ms ease-out)
- Add stronger shadow/glow to the focused card
- Ensure the effect works on both desktop hover and is accessible
- The focused card should clearly stand out from the rest

This creates a clear visual hierarchy directing attention to the specific experience being explored.

Update case studies content:

1. GTM & Market Expansion case study:
   - Remove: 'vetted U.S. compliance requirements'
   - Add instead: 'researched banking partners'
   - Keep the rest of the content the same

2. Cross-Functional Operations Leadership case study:
   - Remove: 'financial model'
   - Add instead: 'made banking and insurance tie-ups'
   - Keep the rest of the content the same

Ensure the changes fit naturally into the existing bullet structure and maintain consistent tone.

Fix spotlight and zoom issues in hero section:

1. Fix spotlight being cut off:
   - The spotlight is being clipped/cut in half on the name and headline
   - Ensure parent containers have overflow: visible (not hidden or clip)
   - Check if any container has restrictive dimensions cutting off the spotlight
   - The spotlight mask/gradient should fully cover the text without being cropped
   - Increase spotlight container size if needed
   - Test that spotlight is fully visible when hovering over any part of the name or headline

2. Fix zoom effect on scroll:
   - The name 'Keerthan Singhvi' should zoom in (scale up) when user scrolls near it
   - Implement proper scroll listener or Intersection Observer
   - Add smooth scale animation to the name element
   - Scale from 1.0 to ~1.05-1.08 when in viewport/during scroll
   - Should work smoothly as user scrolls up and down
   - Add appropriate transition timing (400-600ms ease-out)

Debug both issues thoroughly and ensure the effects work as intended.

CRITICAL FIX for spotlight and zoom issues:

1. Spotlight is still being cut off (see attached screenshot showing spotlight not covering full text):
   - The spotlight effect needs to be applied at a higher container level
   - Remove overflow-hidden or overflow-clip from ALL parent containers in the hero section
   - The spotlight container needs much more padding/space around it
   - Consider applying spotlight effect to the entire hero section background instead of just the text elements
   - Increase the spotlight rendering area significantly
   - The spotlight should smoothly cover the entire name 'KEERTHAN SINGHVI' and the full headline without any clipping
   - Test by moving cursor slowly across the entire text area

2. Zoom effect is completely not working:
   - Remove current broken implementation
   - Add a simple, working scroll-triggered zoom:
     - Use window scroll event listener
     - When user scrolls and the name element is in viewport, apply scale transform
     - Simple CSS transform: scale(1.05) or scale(1.06)
     - Add smooth transition on the element
     - Should be visible when scrolling up/down past the hero
   - Alternative: Add zoom on hover instead of scroll if scroll detection is problematic
   - Make it obvious and working

Reference image: /home/user/Attachments/442356ad-9af5-4607-96bc-0b476a548d66.png shows spotlight cut off on both name and headline.

Fix these thoroughly - both features should be clearly visible and working.

Fix the zoom effect on the name - it's completely not working:

1. Remove the current broken scroll-based zoom implementation
2. Implement a working zoom effect with at least 2x scale:
   - When user scrolls and the hero section is in view, the name 'Keerthan Singhvi' should zoom in
   - Scale should be at least 2.0x (scale(2.0)) or larger
   - Use a simple scroll listener or Intersection Observer
   - Apply transform: scale(2.0) or higher to the name element
   - Add smooth transition (600-800ms ease-out)
   - Should trigger when scrolling near/past the hero
   - Make it very obvious and visible

3. Alternative if scroll detection is problematic:
   - Add zoom on hover over the name element
   - Scale to 2.0x when hovering
   - Smooth transition

The zoom must be clearly visible and working. Test it thoroughly. At least 2x scale as requested.

Change zoom behavior - it should trigger on spotlight/hover, NOT on scroll:

1. Remove the scroll-triggered zoom completely
2. Implement hover-triggered zoom:
   - When user hovers over 'Keerthan Singhvi' name (same time spotlight activates), the name should zoom in
   - Scale to at least 2.0x (transform: scale(2.0)) when hovering
   - Smooth transition (400-600ms ease-out)
   - Should zoom in when cursor enters the name area
   - Should zoom back out when cursor leaves
   - Works in sync with the spotlight effect

3. Ensure the zoom doesn't clip:
   - Add transform-origin: center
   - Parent container should allow the scaled element to expand
   - May need overflow: visible on parent

The zoom should ONLY happen when spotlighting/hovering the name, not during scroll.

Add interactive image reveal in 'The Operator' About section:

1. Add section transition/entrance animation (smooth fade-in or slide-in when scrolling to this section)

2. Below the 'The Operator' title, add an image container with dual-layer reveal effect:
   - Top layer: Your professional photo (/home/user/Attachments/b2345b8d-ee02-4ad1-b4d7-7f370dd21664.png)
   - Bottom layer: Batman image (/home/user/Attachments/eb933df0-0d6a-4316-989a-f46efeb8248e.png)

3. Implement cursor-driven reveal effect:
   - When user hovers over your photo, a circular spotlight/mask follows the cursor
   - The spotlight reveals the Batman image underneath at the cursor position
   - Similar to the hero spotlight but creates a 'cutout' effect revealing the layer below
   - Spotlight radius: ~120-150px with smooth feathered edges
   - Smooth cursor tracking with slight lag (~80-100ms)
   - The rest of the photo remains normal (your professional image visible)

4. Image styling:
   - Appropriate size (not too large, maybe 300-400px width or height)
   - Rounded corners or circular frame
   - Positioned centrally or aligned well with the text content
   - Professional presentation
   - Add subtle border or shadow for depth

5. Technical implementation:
   - Use CSS clip-path, mask-image, or canvas for the reveal effect
   - Ensure smooth performance (GPU-accelerated)
   - Both images should be same dimensions and aligned perfectly
   - Add cursor: pointer to indicate interactivity

This creates a fun easter egg interaction while maintaining professional aesthetics.

Fix image proportions and alignment in The Operator section:

1. Replace the images with cropped versions:
   - Professional photo: /home/user/keerthan-cropped.png (belt and Gemini logo removed)
   - Batman photo: /home/user/batman-cropped.png (cropped to match)
   - Both are now 558x750px (better proportions)

2. Fix alignment and layout:
   - The image container should be better aligned with the text content
   - Adjust image size to be more proportional to the section (maybe 320-360px width)
   - Ensure proper vertical alignment with the text paragraphs
   - Center the image properly in its container
   - Adjust spacing between image and text for better balance
   - The image should feel integrated with the content, not floating awkwardly

3. Visual refinement:
   - Maintain rounded corners and shadow
   - Keep the hover reveal effect working smoothly
   - Ensure 'hover to reveal' hint is positioned well
   - The overall section should feel balanced and professional

The layout from the screenshot shows the image is too tall/narrow - adjust dimensions and positioning for better visual balance.

Refine alignment and spatial balance in the About section only:

**Grid Alignment:**
- Align the left edge of all these elements to the same vertical grid line:
  - '01 — ABOUT' label
  - 'The Operator' headline
  - Photo card
  - Left column container
- Remove any extra left padding inconsistency
- Ensure clean vertical alignment throughout

**Vertical Alignment:**
- Align the top of the photo card with the baseline of the first paragraph block on the right
- Reduce vertical offset between 'The Operator' headline and the photo/content below it
- They should read as one visual unit
- Avoid large empty gaps between headline and content

**Column Balance:**
- Reduce excessive empty space below the photo card
- Either:
  - Slightly increase photo card height, OR
  - Vertically center the photo within the section container
- Left and right columns should feel visually balanced in height
- Photo shouldn't look like it's floating with too much space below

**Status Row Positioning:**
- The status row ('Open to Opportunities · Bangalore, India') should be attached to the right text column baseline
- Maintain consistent left alignment with the paragraph column
- Add slight top spacing but keep it visually connected to the About block
- Should feel like it belongs to the text content

**Optical Spacing:**
- Adjust padding and margins using optical balance rather than equal pixel spacing
- Ensure the composition feels centered and grounded, not drifting left
- The section should feel harmonious and intentionally composed

**DO NOT:**
- Change typography, colors, or content
- Modify the interactive hover reveal effect
- Change the overall layout structure

Focus purely on refinement of spacing, alignment, and visual balance.

Final optical balance and editorial alignment refinement for About section:

**Image and Text Baseline Alignment:**
- Align the top edge of the photo card EXACTLY with the baseline of the first paragraph block on the right
- Remove any residual vertical offset between image and text start positions
- Both columns should start at precisely the same vertical position
- Maintain consistent vertical rhythm across both columns
- No gap or offset at the top of the content area

**Status Row Anchoring:**
- The status row ('Open to Opportunities · Bangalore, India') should be attached to the right text column alignment
- Left-align it with the paragraph text column (not the section container)
- Add modest top spacing (maybe mt-6 or mt-8) so it reads as part of the About content rather than a disconnected footer
- Should feel connected to the text above it

**Visual Weight Balancing:**
- Slightly reduce the photo card shadow strength or blur radius
  - Current is shadow-xl shadow-black/25, maybe reduce to shadow-lg shadow-black/20
- Reduce any heavy vignette or edge darkening near the image area
- Ensure left and right columns feel visually balanced in brightness and density
- Photo shouldn't feel too heavy or dark compared to text side

**Optical Polish:**
- Verify consistent horizontal margins across:
  - Section label ('01 — ABOUT')
  - Headline ('The Operator')
  - Image container
  - Text paragraphs
- Make micro adjustments for optical centering rather than equal pixel spacing
- The composition should feel intentionally balanced and grounded

**DO NOT:**
- Change typography, colors, or content
- Modify layout structure
- Change interaction behavior (hover reveal effect)

This is final polish - focus on subtle optical refinements for editorial-grade alignment.

Redesign the About section layout to make everything fit together harmoniously. You have full freedom to resize and adjust:

Reference image: /home/user/Attachments/80d6453c-9968-42eb-9d69-b89b06a5a35a.png

Current issues:
- Photo seems too tall/large for the space
- Text column feels cramped
- Overall proportions feel off

Free to adjust:
- Photo dimensions (make smaller if needed)
- Title size
- Column widths and gaps
- Spacing and padding
- Grid structure
- Anything else needed for visual balance

Goals:
- Make the section feel cohesive and balanced
- Photo and text should complement each other in size
- Comfortable reading experience
- Professional, editorial layout quality
- Everything should fit together naturally

Keep:
- The interactive hover reveal effect
- Typography styles (fonts, colors)
- Content
- Overall dark theme

Focus on making this section look great - use your design judgment to resize and rebalance as needed.

Restructure the About section layout so '01 — ABOUT' and the first paragraph ('Most of the interesting things...') are on the same horizontal line, side by side:

New layout structure:
- '01 — ABOUT' should be positioned on the left
- First paragraph text ('Most of the interesting things...') should start on the same baseline to the right of it
- They should be horizontally aligned

Optimize the entire section layout:
- Rearrange the grid/flex structure to support this horizontal alignment
- 'The Operator' title should probably move below or be integrated differently
- Photo and text content should still be well-balanced
- Maintain the interactive hover reveal effect on the photo
- Keep the status row at the bottom

Consider layouts like:
- Label on far left, then title + content in main area, OR
- Label inline with first paragraph, title above both, OR
- Another editorial layout that makes this work elegantly

Goals:
- Clean horizontal alignment of label and text start
- Professional editorial layout
- Everything fits together naturally
- Maintain visual hierarchy
- Keep responsive design working

Use your design judgment to make this layout work beautifully.

Refactor the About section layout for improved clarity, scale, and visual balance:

**Layout Structure:**
- Simple two-column grid
- Left column: Portrait image + section label ('01 — ABOUT') + title ('The Operator') grouped with image
- Right column: All paragraph text + status row
- Columns should feel balanced in visual weight

**Image Sizing (CRITICAL):**
- Increase portrait image size by ~30-40% from current size
- Image should feel confident and present, not like a thumbnail
- Maintain rectangular shape and rounded corners
- Image height should visually match the height of the first 2-3 paragraphs
- Do NOT crop tighter on the face
- Make it substantial and intentional

**Title Placement (Option A preferred):**
- Place '01 — ABOUT' and 'The Operator' directly ABOVE the image in the left column
- Stack them as a compact header block
- Keep orange underline under 'The Operator'
- Alternative: Place title below image as caption-style headline
- Choose whichever creates stronger visual balance
- Avoid floating the title separately from the image

**Alignment Rules:**
- Align the top of the image with the top of the text block (right column)
- Ensure consistent left alignment between image, title block, and left column container
- Avoid excessive left padding

**Spacing & Balance:**
- Reduce unused empty space in the left column
- Keep comfortable but tight spacing between title → image and image → text
- Layout should feel centered and grounded
- Eliminate awkward negative space

**Status Row:**
- Keep status row aligned with the text column
- Position directly under the last paragraph
- Maintain visual connection to About copy

**DO NOT:**
- Shrink the image (increase it instead!)
- Split into more than two columns
- Add decorative elements
- Change text content, colors, typography, or interactions

**Goal:** Simple, confident, magazine-like layout. Photo should feel intentional and strong. Layout should look obvious rather than clever.

Refine the About section layout:

1. **Push paragraphs to the right:**
   - Increase gap between left and right columns (maybe gap-16 or gap-20)
   - Or add left margin to the text column
   - Create more breathing room between image and text

2. **Keep 'The Operator' in one line:**
   - Ensure the title doesn't wrap
   - May need to reduce font size slightly or adjust width
   - Use text-nowrap or whitespace-nowrap if needed

3. **Increase image size accordingly:**
   - Since we're adding more gap, we can make the image larger
   - Increase width to maybe 320-360px
   - Make it more substantial and confident
   - Should fill the left column better

4. **Trim content by 1-2 lines:**
   - Remove the shortest paragraph or combine sentences
   - Look at the last paragraph ('Still a lot of figuring out left to do.') - consider removing it
   - Or trim from other paragraphs
   - Goal: tighter, more impactful copy that fits the layout better
   - Make the text block shorter to better match the image height

Goal: Cleaner separation between left and right, larger more confident image, tighter copy, everything feels balanced and intentional.

Align the right-side text block vertically with the left column in the About section:

**Vertical Alignment Requirements:**
1. **Top alignment:** The top of the first paragraph should align with the top of 'The Operator' heading
   - First paragraph and title should start at the same vertical position
   
2. **Bottom alignment:** The bottom of the text (including the 'Open to Opportunities' status line) should align with the bottom edge of the photo
   - Status row should be at the same vertical level as the bottom of the image
   - The entire text block (paragraphs + status) should fill the vertical space from the title top to the photo bottom

3. **Fill the space evenly:** Adjust typography to make text fill this vertical space naturally:
   - Increase font size slightly if needed
   - Adjust line height (leading) for better spacing
   - Adjust paragraph spacing (space between paragraphs)
   - The goal is for the text to comfortably fill from top to bottom without looking cramped or too sparse

**Maintain:**
- Current horizontal gap between photo and text columns (gap-20)
- All content and interactions
- Color and typography styles

**Goal:** Perfect vertical alignment where the text block and the left column (title + photo) have matching top and bottom boundaries. The text should flow naturally within this constrained height.

Reduce zoom on the hero name and title, and make it travel/break letter by letter:

1. **Reduce zoom scale:**
   - Current hover zoom on 'Keerthan Singhvi • Founder's Office' is 2.0x
   - Reduce to 1.3x or 1.4x (something more subtle)
   - Keep smooth transition

2. **Add letter-by-letter zoom animation:**
   - Instead of zooming the entire text at once, make the zoom travel through each letter/character
   - As cursor moves across the text, each letter should zoom individually
   - Create a wave/ripple effect where letters zoom as the cursor passes over them
   - Letters should return to normal size after cursor passes
   - This creates a 'breaking' or 'traveling' effect through the text
   - Use JavaScript to wrap each character in a span and apply individual transforms based on cursor position
   - Should feel fluid and responsive to cursor movement

**Technical approach:**
- Split text into individual character spans
- Track cursor position relative to each character
- Apply scale transform to characters near the cursor
- Use distance-based scaling (closer = larger scale)
- Smooth transitions on each character

Increase the size of the reveal circle on the Batman photo interaction:

- Current reveal circle is 100px radius
- Increase to 150-180px radius for a larger interaction area
- Make it easier to reveal the Batman image underneath
- Keep the smooth cursor tracking and feathered edges
- Maintain the same hover behavior and animation quality

The larger circle will make the easter egg more discoverable and enjoyable.

Replace the About section paragraph content with new text. Keep all layout, alignment, spacing, and styling exactly the same - only change the text content:

New paragraphs:

1st paragraph:
Most of the interesting things in my career started with low expectations and a lot of curiosity. Including my first real job. That combination has quietly become my default mode for figuring things out.

2nd paragraph:
I landed in the Founder's Office at a construction tech startup and quickly learned that the job description was more of a loose suggestion. One week I was mapping customer workflows, the next I was talking to vendors, running GTM experiments, or untangling internal processes that had outgrown their early shortcuts. A lot of it was zero to one. No playbook, imperfect information, and enough pressure to force clarity.

3rd paragraph:
My background is in finance, so I naturally think in tradeoffs, constraints, and what actually moves the needle. But my bias is toward execution. I enjoy building systems that scale cleanly, processes that hold up under pressure, and documentation that people actually use.

4th paragraph:
Somewhere along the way, I realized I'm most useful when things are messy, ambiguous, and slightly chaotic. That's where Batman operates best too. If there's a half-formed problem, unclear ownership, and a ticking deadline, I'm usually happy figuring things out. If needed, I'll fire up the Batmobile and be there. ETA depends entirely on Bangalore traffic.

Do NOT change layout, alignment, spacing, or any visual styling - only replace the text content.

Increase the highlight/visibility on the 'hover to reveal' text on the Batman photo:

- Current text likely has low opacity or subtle styling
- Increase opacity (to 0.7-0.8 or higher)
- Potentially increase font size slightly
- Add more contrast or make it more visible
- Could add a subtle background or glow effect
- Make it more prominent so users notice the interactive feature
- Should still fade out gracefully when hovering

Goal: Make the hint text more noticeable so users discover the Batman easter egg more easily.

Make the Experience section interactive with click-to-expand behavior:

**Default State:**
- Each experience card shows only: Role, Organization, Duration
- Hide: Context line, bullets, skills by default
- Cards should look clickable but not animated by default
- Subtle visual cue that they're interactive (maybe slight border or shadow)

**Hover Behavior:**
- Cursor changes to show semantic cue like 'VIEW IMPACT' or 'OPEN' (custom cursor text or tooltip)
- Card subtly lifts (2-3px translateY) or gains faint outline
- Clear affordance that card is clickable

**Click Behavior:**
- Card expands in place (no modal, no navigation)
- Impact details (context, bullets, skills) fade or slide in smoothly
- Expansion animation originates from the card itself
- Smooth, restrained animation under 300ms

**Rules:**
- Only one experience card can be open at a time
- Clicking another card closes the currently open one with smooth collapse
- Clicking outside the cards (or on open card again) collapses any open card
- No flashy motion, no new colors, no extra copy

**Goal:** Make users choose which experience to explore - active engagement required, not passive scrolling.

**Technical:**
- Use React state to track which card is open
- Smooth height transitions with max-height or CSS grid row animation
- Add click handlers and outside click detection
- Custom cursor styling or tooltip for hover state

Add a persistent section progress indicator:

**Behavior:**
- Thin vertical progress line or minimal section index on the left side of viewport
- Updates smoothly as user scrolls through the site
- Reflects current section state as user enters new sections
- Sections to track: Hero, About, Experience, Case Studies, Skills, Resume, Contact

**Design Constraints:**
- Minimal and unobtrusive
- Respect existing color palette (use accent orange or subtle gray)
- Must not distract from content
- Fixed position on left side (maybe 20-30px from edge)
- Small dots or lines for each section
- Active section highlighted with accent color
- Inactive sections in subtle gray
- No snapping or forced scrolling behavior

**Technical:**
- Use Intersection Observer to detect current section
- Fixed position sidebar with section indicators
- Smooth transitions between active states
- Click on indicators scrolls smoothly to that section (optional enhancement)
- Should make scroll feel guided and intentional

**Goal:** Give users sense of progress and location within the site without being intrusive.

**Visual style:** Minimal dots or short lines vertically stacked, similar to Behance/Awwwards style section indicators.

Remove the section progress indicator (glide dots) completely:

- Remove the fixed left-side vertical progress tracker
- Remove all glide dot navigation elements
- Remove all related code, state management, and intersection observers for the glide dots
- Clean up any leftover styling or positioning

The site should return to simple scroll without any progress indicators.

Highlight the duration (time worked) in the Experience section:

- Make the duration text stand out more visually
- Use accent color (orange #e8793b) or increase font weight/size
- The highlight should be noticeable but not more prominent than the company name
- Company name should remain the most dominant element in the card header
- Duration should be secondary but clearly highlighted
- Could use: accent color text, bold weight, slight size increase, or subtle background pill
- Ensure it works in both collapsed and expanded states

Goal: Make the time worked information more scannable while maintaining visual hierarchy where company name is still primary.
